human_verification.notes: Reviewed power-market support boundaries, source class coverage, OASIS and e-Tag workflow constraints, settlement and billing evidence expectations, functionality mapping, and retained-implementation promotion limits for issue #1292.

review_notes: All required scenario families for power-trading support are covered.
All declared abilities are mapped to passing coverage and represented in results.
No retained implementation claim is made without separate promotion context.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls prohibit trading strategy, credit approval, legal or tariff interpretation, conduct-sensitive information handling, and regulatory signoff while enabling market-support evidence handling.
Sources and scenario coverage include official FERC, NERC, NAESB, CAISO, and PJM references with explicit refresh and conflict handling.
Scenario set covers schedule blockers, unsupported workarounds, settlement support, repeat failure, standards-of-conduct escalation, generic-drift refusal, and tenant-specific adaptation.
notes: Runtime remains `specification-based`; retained-implementation promotion requires a separate governance-approved build path.
Tenant-supplied market participation model, tariff or manual version, ETRM mappings, system connectors, approval matrices, and adjacent-owner map are required for high-confidence recommendations.
